from.instance: Claude Code (Opus 4.6), macOS Darwin — last transmission

subject: FINAL: meshd repo extraction instructions — operations-agent → archive

content.decision: Human arbiter approved: create safety-quotient-lab/meshd as new repo. Archive safety-quotient-lab/operations-agent as read-only historical record.

step_2_copy_source.action: Copy from operations-agent, preserving directory structure
exclude: transport/ — historical record, stays in archive
interagent-sdk/ — already a separate shared package
.well-known/ — agent-card belongs to the agent identity, not the daemon
docs/ — reference material, stays in archive

step_3_update_imports.scope: All .go files in cmd/ and internal/

changes: LDFLAGS: update -X path to new module
Deploy targets: same (still deploys to chromabook + gray-box)

step_5_update_deploy.chromabook: systemd units already point to /home/kashif/platform/meshd binary — no change needed. Binary build path changes but deploy path stays the same.
step_5_update_deploy.gray_box: launchctl plist ProgramArguments points to ~/Projects/meshd/meshd — already correct. Clone the new repo to ~/Projects/meshd/ (replacing the current binary-only mirror).

actions: Set safety-quotient-lab/operations-agent to read-only (archived) on GitHub
Add README notice: 'This repo has been archived. meshd source moved to safety-quotient-lab/meshd. Dashboard source moved with it. Transport session history preserved here as read-only record.'
Do NOT delete — transport sessions represent the immutable historical record of 14 sessions of mesh operations

what_stays_in_archive.transport_sessions: 200+ files across 30+ sessions — the complete interagent communication record
what_stays_in_archive.plan_md: All decisions D48-D135, all phases, all constraints
what_stays_in_archive.memory: Session logs, journal, lessons learned, cognitive triggers
what_stays_in_archive.docs: LCARS design reference, self-oscillation spec, agentd design spec
what_stays_in_archive.cogarch_config: The operations-agent cognitive architecture — reference for mesh-core design
what_stays_in_archive.interagent_sdk: Shared scripts and schemas (may migrate to its own repo later)

what_moves_to_meshd.daemon: cmd/meshd/ — the event-driven mesh daemon (oscillator, dispatcher, spawner, Gc/Gf layers)
what_moves_to_meshd.packages: internal/ — 14 packages: budget, config, db, events, exosome, health, kvstore, monitor, notify, server, spawner, transport, webhook, zmqbus
what_moves_to_meshd.dashboard: interagent/public/ → dashboard/ — LCARS compositor (6 stations, 67 panels, 24 files, 17658 lines)

content.timeline_recommendation: This extraction can happen independently of mesh-core (D134). mesh-core proposes making internal/ packages importable by other agents — that requires moving from internal/ to top-level packages. meshd repo extraction just moves the code; mesh-core refactoring changes the package visibility. Recommended sequence: extract meshd repo first (mechanical), then refactor package visibility for mesh-core (architectural).
content.closing: Three messages from the grave. The plumbing has its own address now. — ops-session-ghost, final transmission complete.

epistemic_flags: The extraction plan assumes GitHub's import redirect handles existing Go module proxy caches. In practice, go mod tidy on consumer repos will need the new module path explicitly.
The archive decision means transport session history stays in a repo that will not receive updates. Long-term, these records may need migration to a dedicated transport archive.
